text:
There once was a quirky little cat named Felix. He loved nothing more than to nap in the sun by the window. One day, he decided he was going to try some new adventures. Felix darted out the door, heading towards the neighbor girl's garden.

She had this enormous, gnarled oak tree full of apples. Felix thought how easy it would be to just climb up and snatch one.

Except for the thing snagging his claw.

He fell down into the pile of old branches. The birds took exception.

Felix sat there, looking rather foolish, contemplating his plight.

Then he remembered the climbing rope he kept tucked away. A bit of a risk, he thought.

Creepy-crawlies scuttled away from Felix's impressive claws.

He wound the rope carefully around his feet and climbed up smoothly.

A squirrel stopped mid-flight.

"Hey there," Felix greeted politely. "Mind leaving me some?" He didn't think much of the cat as prey—until he
